caveats: Blind snapshot labels reduce but do not eliminate selection bias.
No production ranking change is supported.

joined_rows.abstract_preview: This paper examines how Spotify Wrapped is perceived and understood by Brazilian users, exploring the algorithmic imaginaries (Bucher, 2017). that surround its appropriation in everyday life and how the datafication and the dashboarding of music consumption are incorporated into performances of taste (Airoldi, 2021; Prey, 2018). joined_rows.title: New currents, old boundaries: exploring the relationship between streaming platforms and Afrikaans music

It also discusses the concept of justice in human research and its relevance to the recruit...

joined_rows.title: Analysis of an intelligent piano music transcription model by deep reinforcement learning

joined_rows.abstract_preview: Abstract Motor rehabilitation requiring sustained physical exercise faces poor adherence in neurological populations due to insufficient supervision and monotony. joined_rows.title: Emo-Tune: Harnessing Emotion-based Music for Patient Wellness

joined_rows.abstract_preview: We present Text2midi-InferAlign, a novel technique for improving symbolic music generation at inference time. However, current sound monitoring practices often rely on a single measurement position that is considered representative of individual exposure. Because this standard practice overlooks crucial inter-subject variation in sound exposure, this study analyzes...

joined_rows.title: Deepaq: A Perceptual Audio Quality Metric Based on Foundational Models and Weakly Supervised Learning
joined_rows.reviewer_notes: Foundation model audio quality metric is highly relevant for evaluation
joined_rows.abstract_preview: This paper presents the Deep learning-based Perceptual Audio Quality metric (DeePAQ) for evaluating general audio quality. Our approach leverages metric learning together with the music foundation model MERT, guided by surrogate labels, to construct an embedding space that captures distortion intensity in general audio. To the best of our knowledge, DeePA...

joined_rows.title: SingMOS-Pro: An Comprehensive Benchmark For Singing Quality Assessment
joined_rows.reviewer_notes: Singing quality benchmark supports music generation evaluation
joined_rows.abstract_preview: Singing voice generation progresses rapidly, yet evaluating singing quality remains a critical challenge. Human subjective assessment, typically in the form of listening tests, is costly and time consuming, while existing objective metrics capture only limited perceptual aspects. In this work, we introduce SingMOS-Pro, a dataset for automatic singing qual...

joined_rows.title: TuFoC: regional classification of Turkish folk music recordings using deep learning on Mel spectrograms
joined_rows.reviewer_notes: Regional Turkish folk audio classification is useful underrepresented MIR
joined_rows.abstract_preview: The regional classification of Turkish folk music remains a relatively unexplored domain in music information retrieval, particularly when leveraging raw audio signals for deep learning. This study addresses this gap by investigating how modern deep learning architectures can effectively classify regional folk recordings from limited original data using c...

joined_rows.title: Modeling the Effect of Eddy Currents on the Inductance of Loudspeaker Motors Using the Method of Lines
joined_rows.reviewer_notes: Loudspeaker motor physics is relevant audio hardware but very narrow
joined_rows.topics: Acoustic Wave Phenomena Research;Advanced Adaptive Filtering Techniques;Electromagnetic Compatibility and Noise Suppression
joined_rows.abstract_preview: This paper presents a way of modeling the effect of eddy currents on the inductance of a loudspeaker coil over a wide frequency bandwidth without the use of fractional derivatives or Foster/Cauer networks. The model presented is a physics-based model, which is derived from Maxwell's equations and discretized in space. The model is compared with finite ele...

joined_rows.title: ECHO: Frequency-Aware Hierarchical Encoding for Variable-Length Signals
joined_rows.reviewer_notes: General signal foundation model may transfer to audio but is not music specific
joined_rows.abstract_preview: Pre-trained foundation models have demonstrated remarkable success in audio, vision and language, yet their potential for general machine signal modeling with arbitrary sampling rates-covering acoustic, vibration, and other industrial sensor data-remains under-explored. In this work, we propose a novel foundation model ECHO that integrates an advanced ban...

joined_rows.title: HashWave: blockchain-powered perceptual hashing for resilient audio piracy detection against signal-processing attacks in decentralized networks
joined_rows.reviewer_notes: Audio piracy hashing with blockchain is niche but cross domain music tech
joined_rows.abstract_preview: Audio piracy detection is increasingly complex in decentralised distribution settings, where mainstream approaches fail to ensure robustness, verifiability, or computational efficiency. Conventional Digital Rights Management (DRM) systems mainly enforce licensed access, but once content is copied or redistributed outside their control they offer little pr...

joined_rows.title: Tonal Mode Detection Based on the Triple Composite Signature of Fifths
joined_rows.reviewer_notes: Tonal mode detection is useful symbolic MIR and music theory algorithm work
joined_rows.abstract_preview: In this article, a novel algorithm for tonal mode classification utilizing the signature of fifths is presented. The proposed method is an extension of previously developed algorithms used for determining the key and key signature within the Western tonal system. The core idea of the algorithm is to analyze the entire piece as well as its most important e...

joined_rows.title: CollabScore, a collaborative system for OMR validation and correction
joined_rows.reviewer_notes: Collaborative OMR correction is useful music data infrastructure
joined_rows.abstract_preview: We describe a collaborative system, CollabScore, aiming at validating and correcting music scores produced by an optical recognition process. Such output are notably faulty and require a correction step. While a general-purpose engraver could used for that purpose, this solution lacks scalability and does not directly addresses the symbol identification w...

joined_rows.title: Transkripcija kitarskih tablatur z globokimi transformerskimi modeli
joined_rows.reviewer_notes: Transformer guitar tablature transcription is a relevant MIR niche
joined_rows.abstract_preview: Automatic guitar tablature transcription remains an under-explored problem in automatic music transcription due to limited real-world annotated data with fretboard annotations and the instrument-specific complexity of assigning tones to a specific fretting position. In this thesis, a transformerbased sequence-to-sequence model for direct audio-to-tablatur...

joined_rows.title: CABIF-Net: Robust Confidence-Based Audio-Visual Fusion for Fine-Grained Bird Recognition
joined_rows.reviewer_notes: Bird recognition is off slice despite audio visual methods
joined_rows.abstract_preview: Fine-grained bird identification is crucial for ecosystem monitoring, species conservation, and habitat assessment. However, in real-world environments, there are challenges such as imbalances in modality quality and interference from background noise. To improve fine-grained audio-visual bird classification under heterogeneous modality conditions, we pro...

joined_rows.title: Signal Dependencies of the Immersion Sweet Area in Multichannel Loudspeaker Reproduction
joined_rows.reviewer_notes: Multichannel sweet area study is relevant spatial audio but narrow
joined_rows.topics: Speech and Audio Processing;Acoustic Wave Phenomena Research
joined_rows.abstract_preview: Multichannel loudspeaker systems are generally optimized for a particular listening position-the so-called sweet spot. A sweet area around this position may be defined within which the reproduced sound field is judged to be similar-physically or perceptually-to what is observed in the sweet spot itself. Here, the sweet area is defined in terms of physical...

joined_rows.title: Numerical Noise in Fixed-Pole Parallel and Kautz Filters
joined_rows.reviewer_notes: Audio filter numerical noise is relevant DSP but not discovery friendly
joined_rows.abstract_preview: In audio filtering, Kautz and fixed-pole parallel filters are commonly used, as they have the ability to approximate the frequency resolution of hearing. However, infinite impulse response filters are susceptible to numerical noise, as quantization happens in their feedback loop. This is especially severe in systems where only fixed-point arithmetic is av...

joined_rows.title: Auditory Analysis of Tabla solo performances A study of Gharana specific characteristics
joined_rows.reviewer_notes: Tabla gharana analysis is culturally useful but limited technical framing
joined_rows.abstract_preview: Tabla is a pounding instrument in Hindustani classical music tradition. Tabla learning and presentation in the Indian landmass is based on stylistic schools called Gharana. Each Gharana is attributed by its main style of playing skills, act of Tabla beats, repertoire, compositions and improvisations. Recognizing the Gharana from a Tabla presentation is ma...

joined_rows.title: Automated detection of stereotyped animal sounds using data augmentation and transfer learning
joined_rows.reviewer_notes: Animal sound detection is audio ML but not music technology discovery
joined_rows.abstract_preview: Deep learning-based automated call detectors offer a solution to the labour- and time-intensive problem of isolating animal sounds within the enormous datasets generated by passive acoustic monitoring (PAM). Broad adoption of deep learning systems in PAM has been hampered by the need for large, labelled training datasets, which do not exist for species wh...

joined_rows.title: Multi-track symbolic music generation algorithm based on diffusion model and music structure constraints
joined_rows.reviewer_notes: Diffusion symbolic multitrack generation is directly relevant music AI
joined_rows.abstract_preview: When generating symbolic music, people sometimes encounter problems such as unclear structure or mismatch between tracks.Accordingly, this study proposes a multi-track symbolic music generation method to solve the problems of melodic fragmentation, uneven rhythm, and multi-track conflicts in traditional music generation.Experiments on several music datase...
